title: cupaniopsis serrata var. tomentella
intro: Cupaniopsis serrata var. tomentella, also known as the hairy cupaniopsis or hairy garrya, is a plant species within the Sapindaceae family, native to Australia and New Guinea. Traditionally, the leaves and bark of Cupaniopsis serrata var. tomentella have been used in herbal medicine, boasting medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include relief from fever, coughs, and colds, as well as the treatment of skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, Cupaniopsis serrata var. tomentella can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, often in combination with other medicinal plants to enhance its medicinal properties.
